backgroundImageAltText: Bear Team command structure: Bethanne 'Beth' Baer (Broker/Owner) on the left and Tom Songer (Team Lead) on the right, with Emergency Escalation and Open Door Policy badges at the bottom.

params.text:
📞 407-228-1112
✉ Bethanne@bearteam.com
🕐 Mon–Fri business hours; emergencies anytime

CALL BETH FOR:

  • Contract or compliance questions — before taking any action
  • Appraisal gaps or any deal at risk — same day
  • Client complaints or threat of legal action — IMMEDIATELY (do not respond to client first)
  • Cooperating-agent conflicts — after a second failed attempt to resolve
  • Any situation that feels above your pay grade

NEVER WAIT TO BE INVITED. Beth would rather get a call at 9 PM than find out Monday morning that a deal collapsed over the weekend.

📞 [TOM PHONE — fill in]
✉ [TOM EMAIL — fill in]
🕐 Mon–Fri business hours

CALL TOM FOR:

  • BearTeamOS™ technical issues — within 24 hours
  • Training or Bear Academy questions — within 24 hours
  • Marketing, branding, or listing-presentation questions — within 24 hours
  • "How do I do this?" operational questions
  • Anything that's broken in the system

TOM HANDLES THE OPERATING SYSTEM. Beth handles escalations and risk. Tom handles how the work actually gets done day-to-day.

hotspots.header: ⚠ Emergency Escalation — When the Normal Path Doesn't Apply

params.text:
URGENT + OUTSIDE BUSINESS HOURS = CALL BETH DIRECTLY.

Examples that justify a 9 PM call:

  • A client is threatening legal action
  • A deal will expire or fall apart before morning
  • A safety concern (yours, a client's, or a property's)
  • Anything you can't fix and can't afford to wait on

DO NOT:

  • Email and hope she sees it
  • Try to handle it alone
  • Wait until Monday

Beth's standing rule: she would rather take an inconvenient call than discover a preventable disaster on Monday morning. The brokerage runs on early escalation. Use it.

hotspots.header: 🚪 Open Door Policy — Reaching Out Is the Standard

params.text:
REACHING OUT IS NOT A SIGN OF WEAKNESS. It's how this brokerage operates.

The broker and team lead exist to support you. The cost of not calling is always higher than the cost of calling.

THE BEAR TEAM RULE:

  • If you're 80% sure → call anyway and confirm
  • If you're guessing → call before acting
  • If you've already acted and it might be wrong → call faster

Agents who use this resource produce more, get audited less, and stay longer. Agents who try to figure everything out alone are the ones who burn out and leave.
